summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1974 show highlighted by Jerry’s soaring solo, an incredible Loose Lucy, Urah Beard and a chilled Phil & Ned sequence.
review: The recording delivers an immersive ‘being there’ experience despite some dual‑tracking quirks and a board that isn’t the cleanest. Musically the concert is a treat – Jerry’s guitar work shines, Loose Lucy soars, Urah Beard and the Caution Jam pop, and Phil & Ned provide a laid‑back highlight. Overall the performance feels tight in the first set and more adventurous in the second, with only a few slower moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical performance with many standout moments outweighs modest recording imperfections; consistent 4‑5 star reviews give high confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Board isn’t the cleanest – dual‑tracking creates an unreal feel and some muddiness, but overall listenable and captures a vivid ‘live’ atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is technically tight with limited risk‑taking; second set ramps up energy, delivering several high‑impact jams and a few slower spots that feel less engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Superb, soaring guitar work (especially around the 12:00 mark) with new approaches in intros; overall praised as a ‘killer’ performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Featured in a chilled‑out Phil & Ned sequence and pushes strongly during UJB and Caution Jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides solid rhythm support; noted as pushing it out there alongside the rest of the band.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s organ absent on “It Must Have Been the Roses”; otherwise keyboards are functional but not highlighted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and anticipation of Mickey) drives high energy, especially in Caution Jam and drum breaks; percussion on Seastones adds texture.
